1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-04-17
5	---
6	
7	# PR #5 review follow-ups
8	
9	Non-blocker items surfaced by the multi-agent review of PR #5 (`feat/target-color-input`). Blockers B1–B4 were fixed in the PR itself; items below were explicitly deferred. Grouped by area for batched follow-up. Confidence scores and severities are as reported by the reviewers.
10	
11	## Target color feature (feature-reviewer)
12	
13	### `FormatSelector.tsx` — hardcoded `text-white`
14	
15	- **File:** `app/src/components/FormatSelector/FormatSelector.tsx:31`
16	- **Severity:** Medium
17	- **What:** `const btnActive = "bg-surface-active text-white";` uses raw `text-white` instead of a semantic token.
18	- **Why it matters:** Project rule (user's saved feedback + `.claude/CLAUDE.md`) forbids hardcoded colors on text. `text-text-primary` is 98% lightness — the correct substitute.
19	- **Fix:** change `text-white` → `text-text-primary`.
20	
21	### `TargetSwatch.tsx` — hardcoded `rgba()` in inline `boxShadow`
22	
23	- **File:** `app/src/components/TargetSwatch/TargetSwatch.tsx:269-270`
24	- **Severity:** Medium
25	- **What:**
26	  ```tsx
27	  ? "0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.1), inset 0 0 0 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)"
28	  : "0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.15)",
29	  ```
30	  Two hardcoded `rgba()` shadow values on the swatch circle's drifted/normal states.
31	- **Why it matters:** Project rules prohibit `rgba(` in component styles; tokens must be used.
32	- **Fix:** move to Tailwind `ring-*` / `shadow-*` utilities referencing CSS variable tokens (e.g. `var(--color-border-default)`), or tokenize these as new `--color-ring-*` variables if alpha compositing is required.
33	
34	### `TargetSwatch.tsx` — clear button invisible on keyboard focus
35	
36	- **File:** `app/src/components/TargetSwatch/TargetSwatch.tsx:286-294`
37	- **Severity:** Important (a11y)
38	- **Confidence:** 85
39	- **What:** The clear-target button uses `opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100` — it never appears for keyboard-only users.
40	- **Fix:** add `focus-visible:opacity-100` to the class list (and ideally a visible focus ring when it appears).
41	
42	### `TargetSwatch.tsx` — canvas picker has no touch support
43	
44	- **File:** `app/src/components/TargetSwatch/TargetSwatch.tsx:301-309` (and the mouse-only `useEffect` at ~231-244)
45	- **Severity:** Low / Nit
46	- **Confidence:** 80
47	- **What:** Only `onMouseDown` + document `mousemove`/`mouseup` are wired. No `onTouchStart`/`onTouchMove`/`onTouchEnd`. Keyboard support is now in place (B4), but touch is still missing.
48	- **Why it matters:** Popover works on mobile via the text input, but the 2D picker is unusable with touch.
49	- **Fix:** add Pointer Events (`onPointerDown`/`onPointerMove`/`onPointerUp`) which unifies mouse/touch/pen, or add touch handlers alongside the existing mouse handlers.
50	
51	### `ColorInput` — input missing `aria-label`
52	
53	- **File:** `app/src/components/TargetSwatch/TargetSwatch.tsx:80-92`
54	- **Severity:** Important (a11y)
55	- **Confidence:** 85
56	- **What:** The color input has a placeholder (`#hex, rgb(), hsl(), oklch()`) but no `aria-label`. Placeholders are not accessible names.
57	- **Fix:** add `aria-label="Target color"` (or similar) to the `<input>`.
58	
59	### `TargetFormats.tsx` — no `aria-live` on copy feedback
60	
61	- **File:** `app/src/components/TargetFormats/TargetFormats.tsx:33-51`
62	- **Severity:** Medium (a11y)
63	- **What:** Clicking a format button swaps the label text from e.g. `rgb(180, 90, 127)` to `Copied`. Screen readers get no announcement.
64	- **Fix:** add `aria-live="polite"` and `aria-atomic="true"` to the value `<span>` (around line 47), or wrap the format row in a live region.
65	
66	### `TargetFormats.tsx` — `opacity-55` wrapper degrades contrast
67	
68	- **File:** `app/src/components/TargetFormats/TargetFormats.tsx:27`
69	- **Severity:** Medium (contrast)
70	- **What:** `opacity-55` on a container dims text inside for the drifted state. `text-faint` inside a 55%-opacity wrapper on a raised surface falls well below AA.
71	- **Fix:** don't use container opacity to dim text. Swap to explicit dimmer text tokens for the drifted state, or reduce opacity on the background only.
72	
73	### `TargetFormats.tsx` — `formatAllFormats` not memoized
74	
75	- **File:** `app/src/components/TargetFormats/TargetFormats.tsx:23`
76	- **Severity:** Nit (perf)
77	- **What:** `formatAllFormats(hue, saturation)` calls chroma 4× per render. Any parent re-render triggers redundant conversions on a component visible during every palette interaction.
78	- **Fix:** `const formats = useMemo(() => formatAllFormats(hue, saturation), [hue, saturation]);`
79	
80	### `lib/colors.ts` — oklch regex rejects slash-alpha syntax
81	
82	- **File:** `app/src/lib/colors.ts:24-27`
83	- **Severity:** Low
84	- **What:** The regex `/oklch\(\s*([\d.]+%?)\s+([\d.]+)\s+([\d.]+)\s*\)/i` rejects modern CSS Color Level 4 alpha like `oklch(0.7 0.15 200 / 0.5)`. Users pasting from modern design tools get a false parse failure.
85	- **Fix:** extend the regex to optionally match `\s*(?:\/\s*[\d.]+%?)?\s*` before the closing paren. Ignore the alpha channel for hue/sat extraction (tool operates at fixed L=50).
86	
87	### `lib/colors.ts` — HSL/RGB output missing spaces after separators
88	
89	- **File:** `app/src/lib/colors.ts:69, 72`
90	- **Severity:** Low / Nit
91	- **What:** Emits `hsl(180,50%,50%)` and `rgb(180,90,127)` — no spaces after commas. Valid legacy CSS but deviates from DevTools output and the OKLCH formatter (which uses spaces). Feels rough on copy.
92	- **Fix:** `hsl(${hue}, ${Math.round(saturation * 100)}%, 50%)` and `rgb(${r}, ${g}, ${b})`.
93	
94	## Tooling (tooling-reviewer)
95	
96	### Port SSOT violation
97	
98	- **Files:** `scripts/config.sh:6`, `app/vite.config.ts:9`
99	- **Severity:** High
100	- **What:** The `5173` fallback for `PORT` is duplicated in both files. The real source of truth is `app/.env.development.local`.
101	- **Why it matters:** User has explicit saved feedback against SSOT violations. If the default changes, two files must be updated.
102	- **Fix:** drop one of the fallbacks (prefer keeping the Vite default and removing from `config.sh`), or centralize in a single env read.
103

105	
106	- **File:** `scripts/config.sh:5-6`

108	
109	### ~~Root `package-lock.json` has ghost `prettier-eslint` entry~~ — RESOLVED in PR #5
110	
111	- **File:** root `package-lock.json`
112	- **Resolution:** regenerated via `npm install`. 152 ghost packages removed; 0 vulnerabilities.
113	
114	### 26 auto-fixable `better-tailwindcss` warnings
115	
116	- **Severity:** Low
117	- **What:** `npm run verify` exits 0 but emits 26 warnings across several components (canonical class simplifications like `h-4 w-4` → `size-4`, one duplicate class).
118	- **Fix:** `cd app && npm run lint:fix` (or equivalent).

120	
121	### `contrast-audit.mjs` not wired into any npm script
122	
123	- **File:** `scripts/contrast-audit.mjs`
124	- **Severity:** Low
125	- **What:** Script exists but no `contrast`/`audit` entry in `app/package.json` or root `package.json`.
126	- **Fix:** add a script entry if it's intended for CI / verify; otherwise document as a manual dev tool.
127	
128	### Dead ESLint override globs

131	- **Severity:** Nit
132	- **What:** Override includes `src/pages/**/*.astro`, `astro.config.*`, `next.config.*` — this is a React/Vite app with no Astro or Next.
133	- **Fix:** trim the dead globs.
134	
135	### No `.nvmrc` / `engines` field

137	- **Severity:** Nit
138	- **What:** Global CLAUDE.md prefers Node ^24 LTS; `@types/node` is pinned to `^24.12.0` but there's no `.nvmrc` or `engines` constraint.
139	- **Fix:** add `.nvmrc` with `24` and/or `"engines": { "node": ">=24" }` to `app/package.json`.
140	
141	## Design tokens (tokens-reviewer)

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./board/planning/NOTE-0031_tailwind-11-swatch-preset.md`

```text
1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-04-17
5	---
6	
7	# Tailwind 11-swatch preset
8	
9	Add a swatch-count option for **11 swatches** that explicitly maps to Tailwind's shade scale: `50, 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 900, 950`.
10	
11	## Why
12	
13	Tailwind 3+ uses 11 shades per color family. Designers building Tailwind-compatible palettes currently have to either count manually or export and relabel. A first-class "11 (Tailwind)" preset makes the tool land directly in the workflow of anyone writing `@theme` config or `tailwind.config.js`.
14	
15	## Scope
16	
17	- Swatch count option in the layout/controls alongside existing counts.
18	- Label: "11 (Tailwind)" or "Tailwind" — keep it short, put the scale hint in a tooltip.
19	- Swatches rendered in Tailwind's canonical order: `50 → 950` (lightest → darkest).
20	- Lightness distribution should match Tailwind's non-uniform spacing (50 and 950 are edge cases — 50 is very light, 950 is very dark; the interior steps are roughly linear).
21	  - Rough Tailwind L values (approx, check reference palettes): 50: 97%, 100: 93%, 200: 86%, 300: 77%, 400: 66%, 500: 55%, 600: 47%, 700: 39%, 800: 31%, 900: 24%, 950: 16%.
22	  - These aren't evenly spaced — 50 is far from 100, and 950 is far from 900. The parabolic saturation curve needs to still produce balanced output at these lightness values.
23	- Export should label each swatch with its Tailwind number (`50`, `100`, ... `950`) instead of just an index.
24	
25	## Open questions
26	
27	- **Lightness mapping**: Should the preset lock lightness values to Tailwind's exact scale (so palettes always match Tailwind's perceptual spacing), or keep the existing "start/end lightness + equal distribution" and just count to 11? The former is more useful as a preset; the latter is simpler.
28	- **Naming vs. numbering**: Always show `50/100/.../950` labels in the UI when the preset is active, even outside export? Might be useful for at-a-glance verification.
29	- **Related to NOTE-0007 (Named swatches)**: a full "named swatches" system could subsume this — the Tailwind preset is one instance of a named scale. Worth considering jointly if that work lands first.
30	- **Related to NOTE-0021 (Custom swatch intervals)**: custom intervals would let users define arbitrary scales (including Tailwind-like). The 11-swatch Tailwind preset is the curated default that ships with the tool.
31	- **Export formats**: CSS custom properties should emit `--color-gray-50: #…;` ... `--color-gray-950: #…;` naming automatically when this preset is active.
32	
33	## References
34	
35	- Tailwind default color palette: https://tailwindcss.com/docs/colors
36	- Related notes: NOTE-0007 (Named swatches), NOTE-0021 (Custom swatch intervals), NOTE-0024 (Sync chart step dots with swatch count)
37
```
</details></tool-use>

1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox

5	---
6	
7	# Bring back "show black and white swatches" option
8	
9	We used to have a "B/W swatches" toggle that prepended pure white (L=100) and appended pure black (L=0) to the generated palette as bookends. It was removed in commit `320c440` ("feat(palette): add lightness range slider with lock, replace B/W toggle") and replaced by the dual-thumb lightness range slider.
10	
11	## Why bring it back
12	
13	The range slider is strictly more flexible, but it doesn't cleanly reproduce the old behavior. Users wanted pure #000 and #FFF as framing anchors alongside a chromatic palette — not to drag L bounds all the way to 0/100, which:
14	
15	- Still produces **colored** endpoints (the saturation curve pushes nonzero chroma at near-black and near-white).
16	- Makes the chromatic interior narrower — you lose resolution in the useful midtones.
17	- Requires two separate drags; it's not a one-click affordance.
18	
19	A dedicated B/W toggle gives a different output shape: a fully chromatic scale at its current bounds, *flanked* by true neutral anchors. That's genuinely useful for:
20	
21	- Tailwind-style scales where `white` (#fff) and `black` (#000) sit outside the numbered shades.
22	- Design tokens that want explicit neutral endpoints regardless of hue/sat settings.
23	- Exports where the consumer expects `white`/`black` as named tokens.
24	
25	## Design questions to think through
26	
27	- **Count semantics**: If the user has "swatches = 8" and toggles B/W on, does the grid show 8 or 10 total? (The old behavior added 2 extras; modern counts like Tailwind-11 already include `50`/`950`, so the answer depends on whether B/W is bookends *outside* or labels replacing the endpoints.)
28	- **Interaction with lightness range**: What if the user has set L bounds to e.g. 10–90? Do the B/W bookends still appear (as true 0/100) regardless of the current range? Probably yes — that's the whole point — but worth confirming.
29	- **Interaction with Tailwind-11 preset (NOTE-0031)**: The Tailwind scale's `50` and `950` aren't pure white/black. So B/W-on-top-of-Tailwind would give 13 swatches: `white, 50, 100, ..., 950, black`. Acceptable? Or mutually exclusive?
30	- **Export labeling**: Bookend swatches should export as `white` / `black` (or `0` / `100`), not be shoved into the numeric sequence. The old code had a `label` field per entry — use that.
31	- **Placement in UI**: Before it was a small toggle in the ControlBar alongside layout/swatches. Options:
32	  - Tiny toggle next to the swatch-count select (original pattern).
33	  - Part of a "scale options" group if Tailwind preset lands.
34	  - Inside the layout popover, de-emphasized.
35	- **Naming**: "Show black and white," "Include B/W anchors," "Add endpoints"? Old name was fine but terse.
36	- **Default state**: Off (current behavior) or on-by-default for new users? Probably off — additive feature, don't change defaults.
37	
38	## Related
39	
40	- NOTE-0007 (Named swatches) — B/W anchors are a special case of named-scale support.
41	- NOTE-0021 (Custom swatch intervals) — if users can define arbitrary scales, "B/W bookends" is a special case.
42	- NOTE-0031 (Tailwind 11-swatch preset) — decide whether B/W stacks with Tailwind or is mutually exclusive.
43	
44	## References
45	
46	- Feature introduced: commit `4024721` ("feat(palette): add layout options, B/W swatches, and improve grid defaults")
47	- Feature removed: commit `320c440` ("feat(palette): add lightness range slider with lock, replace B/W toggle")
48
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./board/planning/NOTE-0033_migrate-to-pnpm-workspaces.md`

```text
1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-04-17
5	---
6	
7	# Migrate to pnpm + workspaces
8	
9	Convert the repo from npm (single `app/` package, root-as-passthrough) to pnpm with a declared workspace. Small change, big payoff — unblocks future sibling packages (`site/`, `api/`, shared `tokens/`) without restructure, replaces awkward `npm run --prefix app` with first-class `pnpm --filter app` syntax, and gives us a single hoisted store with a deterministic lockfile.
10	
11	## Why pnpm (not npm workspaces)
12	
13	- **Strict by default.** pnpm doesn't flatten `node_modules` — workspaces can't accidentally import each other's transitive deps. Matters once we add a second package.
14	- **Content-addressed store.** Disk-efficient across projects; faster cold installs.
15	- **First-class workspace CLI.** `pnpm --filter <name>`, `pnpm -r`, `pnpm -w` are ergonomic. npm's `--workspace` / `-w` works but is younger and has rougher edges around deploy tooling.
16	- **Global tooling precedent.** User default is npm for standalone projects; pnpm is the pragmatic upgrade when workspaces enter the picture (see EPIC-0013 which already adds `site/`).
17	
18	## Current shape
19	
20	- Root `package.json`: private, no deps except `wrangler`, 15+ scripts that shell into `npm run --prefix app <cmd>`.
21	- `app/package.json`: the actual React/Vite/TS app. Has its own `package-lock.json`.
22	- Shell scripts in `scripts/` call `npm run` (run-all.sh, run-sequential.sh, dev.sh).
23	- `wrangler.jsonc` at root governs Cloudflare Pages deploy; `deploy` script runs `npm run --prefix app build && wrangler pages deploy`.
24	- `.gitignore` already ignores `**/node_modules/` — no change needed.
25	
26	## Target shape
27	
28	```
29	/
30	├── pnpm-workspace.yaml     # packages: ['app']
31	├── pnpm-lock.yaml          # single unified lockfile
32	├── package.json            # packageManager: "pnpm@9.x", root scripts use --filter
33	├── app/
34	│   ├── package.json        # unchanged except lockfile removed
35	│   └── (no package-lock.json, no pnpm-lock.yaml — one lockfile at root)
36	└── scripts/*.sh            # use pnpm run instead of npm run
37	```
38	
39	## Plan (adapted from previous Claude's npm draft)
40	
41	### 1. Declare the workspace
42	
43	Add `pnpm-workspace.yaml` at repo root:
44	
45	```yaml
46	packages:
47	  - app
48	```
49	
50	Add `packageManager` field to root `package.json` (enables Corepack pinning):
51	
52	```json
53	{
54	  "packageManager": "pnpm@9.15.0"
55	}
56	```
57	
58	(Pin to whatever the latest stable is at migration time.)
59	
60	### 2. Clean slate
61	
62	```bash
63	rm -rf node_modules app/node_modules
64	rm package-lock.json app/package-lock.json
65	```
66	
67	### 3. Reinstall
68	
69	```bash
70	pnpm install
71	```
72	
73	Generates a single `pnpm-lock.yaml` at root. Commit it.
74	
75	### 4. Migrate root scripts to `--filter` syntax
76	
77	Replace every `npm run --prefix app <cmd>` with `pnpm --filter app run <cmd>` (or `pnpm -F app <cmd>` — shorter alias).
78	
79	```json
80	{
81	  "scripts": {
82	    "build": "pnpm -F app build",
83	    "verify": "pnpm -F app verify",
84	    "verify:fix": "pnpm -F app verify:fix",
85	    "verify:strict": "pnpm -F app verify:strict",
86	    "deploy": "pnpm -F app build && wrangler pages deploy",
87	    "dev": "./scripts/dev.sh",
88	    "dev:app": "pnpm -F app dev",
89	    "format": "pnpm -F app format",
90	    "format:fix": "pnpm -F app format:fix",
91	    "knip": "pnpm -F app knip",
92	    "knip:fix": "pnpm -F app knip:fix",
93	    "lint": "pnpm -F app lint",
94	    "lint:fix": "pnpm -F app lint:fix",
95	    "log": "./scripts/log.sh watch",
96	    "log:tail": "./scripts/log.sh tail",
97	    "preview": "pnpm -F app preview",
98	    "status": "./scripts/status.sh",
99	    "stop": "./scripts/stop.sh",
100	    "typecheck": "pnpm -F app typecheck"
101	  }
102	}
103	```
104	
105	### 5. Update shell scripts
106	
107	- `scripts/run-all.sh` and `scripts/run-sequential.sh`: replace `npm run "$task"` with `pnpm run "$task"`. These execute inside `app/` (cwd of the invoking script), so plain `pnpm run` resolves to the app's scripts.
108	- `scripts/dev.sh`: `npm run dev:app` → `pnpm run dev:app`.
109	
110	### 6. Verify end-to-end
111	
112	- `pnpm run dev` — dev server starts on expected port
113	- `pnpm -F app build` — builds clean
114	- `pnpm run verify` — all checks pass
115	- `pnpm run deploy` — dry-run or trusted production deploy; wrangler picks up `app/dist` output
116	- Husky / git hooks (if present) still fire
117	
118	### 7. Update docs
119	
120	- `README.md` — any `npm install` / `npm run …` → `pnpm install` / `pnpm run …`
121	- `.claude/CLAUDE.md` — Commands section references `npm run …`; switch to `pnpm`.
122	- Any board docs referencing npm commands (e.g., TASK-0060 explicitly uses `npm create astro@latest` — scope creep; leave untouched unless we're also converting in-flight work).
123	
124	## Gotchas
125	
126	1. **`packageManager` + Corepack.** With `packageManager: pnpm@x.y.z` set, Corepack-aware environments (Node 16.10+ by default) will refuse to run mismatched pnpm versions. Good for determinism; mildly annoying on machines without Corepack enabled (`corepack enable` fixes it). CI should enable Corepack explicitly.
127	2. **Cloudflare Pages build.** CF Pages detects the package manager from `packageManager` + lockfile automatically. Verify the build config (`wrangler pages deploy` runs locally, but CF Pages CI may run its own install). Check Pages project settings after first pnpm-based deploy.
128	3. **pnpm's strict node_modules.** If anything in `app/` transitively relied on npm's flat hoisting (importing a package not listed as a direct dep), it will break. Typecheck + lint + build should catch it; if not, `pnpm install --shamefully-hoist` is the escape hatch but don't reach for it unless forced.
129	4. **Editor tooling.** Some ESLint/TypeScript plugins resolve deps via `node_modules` lookup. pnpm's symlink layout is well-supported by modern tooling (ESLint 9+, TS 5+, VSCode) but a window reload may be needed after the switch.
130	5. **Husky / lint-staged.** Not currently installed, but if added later they must live in root and reference workspace scripts via `pnpm -F`.
131	6. **`.nvmrc` / `engines`.** Orthogonal to this migration but mentioned in NOTE-0030 — tackle separately or bundle as cleanup at the end.
132	
133	## Out of scope
134	
135	- Splitting `app/` into sub-packages (`tokens/`, `ui/`, etc.) — premature until there's a second consumer.
136	- Adding the Astro `site/` package (EPIC-0013 territory). This migration *enables* that without duplicating lockfiles; the actual addition happens in that epic.
137	- CI config changes beyond what's required for pnpm to install (e.g., Cloudflare Pages build command). Any GitHub Actions / other CI that currently calls `npm` will need a one-line swap — covered by the docs-and-scripts task but worth a scan.
138	
139	## References
140	
141	- EPIC-0013 — adds `site/` (Astro). Will benefit from workspaces landing first.
142	- NOTE-0030 — mentions `.nvmrc` / `engines` addition; adjacent cleanup opportunity.
143
```
</details></tool-use>

16	
17	# EPIC-0006: Migrate palette generation from HSL to OKLCH
18	
19	Migrate all internal color math from HSL to OKLCH. The parabolic correction curve shifts from a necessity (compensating for HSL's perceptual brokenness) to an opt-in artistic refinement. Gamut-aware chroma clamping replaces hard clipping. Hue-dependent center shift makes the curve smarter.
20	
21	## Scope
22	
23	- OKLCH conversion functions (custom, not culori — keep chroma-js for non-generation uses)
24	- Memoized sRGB gamut boundary lookup (per-hue cache, 101 lightness steps)
25	- Hue-dependent chroma correction with both endpoint and target satMode
26	- Hue-aware saturation→chroma slider mapping (100% = max gamut chroma for current hue)
27	- Refactored `generateNeutrals` producing OKLCH internally, deriving HSL/hex at output
28	- Labels stay `grey-{0–100}` (OKLCH lightness scaled to integer)
29	- Visual tuning of default sMod for OKLCH context
30	
31	## Key decisions
32	
33	- **satMode** carries forward (both endpoint and target)
34	- **chroma-js** stays for contrast, export conversion, About — OKLCH math is additive
35	- **sMod/p** parameter mapping unchanged (`pctToSMod` stays as-is)
36	- **Curve defaults to on** — hue-dependent center shift is the new default experience
37	- **About dialog** is a separate deferred effort (EPIC-0008)
38	
39	## Reference
40	
41	- NOTE-0006: Full migration guide with conversion functions, formulas, and checklist
42	- REFE-0001: Parabolic saturation curve background
43
```

 `./board/planning/EPIC-0013_static-site-generation-for-crawlers.md`

```text
1	---
2	type: epic
3	status: planning
4	created: 2026-04-06
5	updated: 2026-04-09
6	---
7	
8	# EPIC-0013: Migrate to Astro for SSG and marketing pages
9	
10	Migrate the app from client-only Vite + React to Astro with React islands. Solves the original crawler-visibility problem (see NOTE-0029) and adds a native content platform for marketing pages.
11	
12	## Context
13	
14	Two drivers converged on this plan:
15	
16	1. **Crawler visibility.** All page content is currently rendered client-side, so crawlers and LLMs see an empty shell. Originally scoped to `vite-react-ssg` in the first version of this epic.
17	2. **Marketing pages.** We want room for `/about`, `/how-it-works`, `/examples`, and eventually a blog. `vite-react-ssg` is a pre-rendering wrapper, not a content platform — Astro is the natural fit and already the preferred content framework per global tooling docs.
18	
19	## Key decisions
20	
21	**Tool-first at `/`.** The palette generator stays at the root URL. A marketing wall between users and the tool would add friction with no payoff — Chroma Curve is a free instrument and the tool *is* the pitch. Marketing content lives at sibling routes (`/about`, `/how-it-works`, etc.). Matches patterns from Coolors, tldraw, Excalidraw, and remove.bg.
22	
23	**SSR'd layout, hydrated island.** The `/` page has an Astro layout (header nav, SEO meta, `sr-only` h1 and description) wrapping a `<PaletteGenerator client:load />` island. Crawlers get real HTML with rich semantics; sighted users get the clean tool interface with a visible path to info pages via the header nav.
24	
25	**`client:load`, not `client:only`.** We want the initial render SSR'd so crawlers see the default palette, not a skeleton. The Zustand store has static defaults and the browser-only code paths are already confined to `useEffect` and event handlers, so this is achievable with a focused hydration audit.
26	
27	**Scaffold alongside, swap at the end.** Build the Astro project in a sibling directory to `app/`, bring it to parity, then swap. Keeps `app/` buildable and shippable throughout the migration.
28	
29	**Dedicated branch.** This is a sizeable restructure and will live on its own branch off `main`, landed separately from any in-flight feature work.
30	
31	## Dependencies
32	
33	- **TASK-0059** (About.tsx refactor into feature directory) is a prerequisite for porting components. Priority bumped from P3 to P1 so it lands before the Astro migration starts. The 912-line monolith is significantly harder to move than 12 modular files.
34	
35	## Tasks
36	
37	### Prerequisite
38	- TASK-0059: Refactor About.tsx into `about/` feature directory *(belongs to EPIC-0004 but blocks TASK-0065)*
39	
40	### Scaffolding and configuration
41	- TASK-0060: Scaffold Astro project alongside `app/`

 `./board/planning/EPIC-0014_target-color-ux-refinements.md`

```text
1	---
2	type: epic
3	status: planning
4	created: 2026-04-06
5	---
6	
7	# EPIC-0014: Target color input UX refinements
8	
9	Users enter a color (e.g., `#FF5733`) into the target picker, but the app extracts only hue + saturation and forces lightness to 50%. The swatch then shows a different hex value, which is confusing — users reasonably wonder "I put in this hex value, why am I seeing a different one?" The "H+S at L50" explanation is small and buried inside a popover with no persistent indicator, and the same confusion extends to HSL and OKLCH displays.
10	
11	## Goals
12	
13	- Make the L50 normalization obvious with clear before/after display of the user's input alongside the resulting working color
14	- Show a persistent "target active" indicator outside the popover so users can see at a glance that they entered a target
15	- Display the working color in all four formats (hex, rgb, hsl, oklch) inline so users can verify the identity and copy any representation
16	- Support a "drift" model: when users move hue/sat sliders after entering a target, the indicator goes stale rather than silently vanishing
17	
18	## Scope
19	
20	- **Move target state to Zustand** — target fields (`targetInput`, `targetFormat`, `targetHue`, `targetSat`) move from local TargetSwatch state to `paletteStore.ts` so `ControlBar` can render the inline multi-format display without prop drilling
21	- **Arrow notation in popover** — replace "Input"/"H+S at L50" with `#FF5733 → #BF4040` and annotation "hue + saturation at L50"
22	- **Persistent target indicator** — swatch trigger shows chromatic ring when target is active, dimmed/dotted when drifted, clear (X) button on hover
23	- **Inline multi-format display** — new `TargetFormats` component in ControlBar showing hex/rgb/hsl/oklch with click-to-copy when target is active
24	- **`formatAllFormats()` utility** — compose existing formatters to return `{ hex, rgb, hsl, oklch }` for a given hue/sat at L50
25	
26	## Deferred
27	
28	Slider-locking (TASK-0055, TASK-0056) was originally in scope to prevent accidental target invalidation, but it pre-emptively solves a problem users don't actually report and risks making the controls feel constrained. Dropping it in favor of the clarity-focused work above. Revisit only if real usage shows users losing targets by accident.
29	
30	## Tasks
31	
32	- TASK-0053
33	- TASK-0054
34	- TASK-0057
35	- TASK-0058
36	- TASK-0055 *(deferred)*
37	- TASK-0056 *(deferred)*
38	
39	## Related
40	
41	- TASK-0046 (done) — initial target color input implementation
42	- NOTE-0013 (archived) — original target color input idea
43	- EPIC-0012 — UX discoverability improvements (related but separate)
44
```
</details></tool-use>

6	
7	# EPIC-0015: Migrate tooling to pnpm workspaces
8	
9	Replace npm with pnpm and declare a workspace at repo root. Root `package.json` becomes a proper workspace manifest; `app/` becomes the first of (eventually) several packages. Unblocks EPIC-0013 (Astro `site/` package) and future sibling packages without a second lockfile or duplicated installs.
10	
11	See **NOTE-0033** for the full plan, rationale, and gotchas.
12	
13	## Goals
14	
15	- Single `pnpm-lock.yaml` at root; no per-package lockfiles.
16	- Root scripts use `pnpm -F app <cmd>` instead of `npm run --prefix app <cmd>`.
17	- All shell scripts under `scripts/` invoke `pnpm` instead of `npm`.
18	- Package manager pinned via `packageManager` field; Corepack-ready.
19	- End-to-end workflow (dev, build, verify, deploy) verified on pnpm before merge.
20	
21	## Scope
22	
23	- Declare workspace (`pnpm-workspace.yaml`, `packageManager` field).
24	- Reset npm state (`node_modules`, both `package-lock.json` files) and reinstall under pnpm.
25	- Migrate root `package.json` scripts to `pnpm --filter` syntax.
26	- Update `scripts/*.sh` (`run-all.sh`, `run-sequential.sh`, `dev.sh`) to call `pnpm`.
27	- Update docs (`README.md`, `.claude/CLAUDE.md`) to reference pnpm commands.
28	- Verify dev server, build, verify, and `wrangler pages deploy` all work under pnpm.
29	
30	## Deferred / out of scope
31	
32	- Splitting `app/` into sub-packages — premature.
33	- Adding `site/` (Astro) package — lives in EPIC-0013; this epic only enables it.
34	- `.nvmrc` / `engines` field — noted in NOTE-0030, bundle separately.
35	- CI config (GitHub Actions etc.) — none currently configured; if added later, use `pnpm`.
36

1	---
2	type: epic
3	status: planning
4	created: 2026-05-06
5	---
6	
7	# EPIC-0016: About dialog narrative refresh
8	
9	The About dialog currently frames Chroma Curve solely as a fix for a perceptual problem — fixed/static saturation produces uneven chroma, and the parabolic curve corrects it. That framing is accurate but incomplete:
10	
11	1. It primes users to interpret the tool as **corrective only**, when the same curve mechanism enables genuinely creative palette shapes (vivid mids, muted mids, asymmetric scales).
12	2. The example labels ("Fixed low saturation," "Fixed high saturation") use *fixed* in the sense of "constant," but in a context all about correcting problems, *fixed* reads ambiguously as "corrected." This is exactly the wrong read.
13	
14	## Goals
15	
16	- Disambiguate the "fixed saturation" labels so the comparison strips clearly mean "constant/static," not "corrected."
17	- Extend the narrative beyond problem-solving to include the creative applications of the curve — both the default smoothing direction and (eventually) the inverted boost direction (TASK-0080).
18	- Keep the existing "show the math" rigor — this is a content layer on top, not a replacement.
19	
20	## Out of scope
21	
22	- The OKLCH rewrite (EPIC-0008 covers reframing for the post-migration mental model).
23	- Visual redesign of the dialog itself — copy and structure only.
24	
25	## Tasks
26	
27	- TASK-0084 — Disambiguate "fixed saturation" labels
28	- TASK-0085 — Add creative applications section
29	
30	## Related
31	
32	- EPIC-0008 — OKLCH rewrite of About dialog (separate, post-migration).
33	- TASK-0080 — Positive (inverted) saturation mode; the creative applications section will reference this once shipped.
34
```
</details></tool-use>

3	status: planning
4	created: 2026-05-06
5	---
6	
7	# EPIC-0017: Control defaults and affordances
8	
9	The curve-shaping controls (hue, saturation, adjustment, lightness range) ship with defaults that don't match the canonical perception-corrected curve, and they offer no clean path back to defaults once the user has tweaked. This makes the *first impression* weaker than it should be (defaults that produce pure black/white endpoints, an unlocked range that drifts asymmetric on the first drag) and the *recovery path* nonexistent (no reset).
10	
11	## Goals
12	
13	- First-load state should reflect the canonical perception-corrected curve — the published default — so a user who opens the app and changes nothing already sees the tool's thesis.
14	- Defaults should bias toward the dominant use case (symmetric range, fully chromatic endpoints), with explicit affordances for the asymmetric/edge cases.
15	- Reset paths exist, are visible only when the control has been modified, and communicate that the default *is* the canonical curve.
16	
17	## Tasks
18	
19	- TASK-0078 — Lock range slider by default
20	- TASK-0079 — Rework lightness range defaults and endpoint handling
21	- TASK-0086 — Add reset-to-default for saturation/adjustment/range
22	
23	## Related
24	
25	- EPIC-0012 — UX discoverability improvements (sibling — discoverability of *what controls do*, vs. this EPIC's focus on *what controls default to and how to recover*).
26	- NOTE-0032 — Bring back B/W bookend swatches (interacts with TASK-0079's endpoint handling).
27	- REFE-0001 — Parabolic saturation curve reference (the canonical "default" lives here).
28
```

# NOTE Inventory Report — Chroma Curve Project Board

### NOTE-0001: Comprehensive Project Review
- **Status**: already-extracted
- **One-line summary**: Full code-quality / a11y / export-system audit produced 38 findings (CQ-1..15, UX-1..16, EX-1..7).
- **Existing coverage**: EPIC-0001 (a11y/ARIA), EPIC-0002 (contrast/legibility), EPIC-0003 (perf), EPIC-0004 (code quality), EPIC-0005 (export polish) — all marked `status: done`. Tasks TASK-0001..0019 cover the catalogued findings.
- **Suggested grouping**: standalone (historical reference)
- **Notes**: The five originating EPICs are already `done`. NOTE remains useful as the source-of-truth audit document but no further extraction needed.

### NOTE-0002: In-context contrast checker
- **Status**: actionable-not-extracted
- **One-line summary**: Add an inline WCAG contrast-pair checker between palette swatches (light text on dark bg, etc.) so users don't bounce to external tools.
- **Existing coverage**: None. EPIC-0002 is about UI-chrome contrast, not user-palette contrast checking.
- **Candidate tasks**:
  - "Design swatch-pair selection UX for contrast comparison" — interaction model (click-to-pin vs hover-pair, where the readout lives).

## Cross-NOTE EPIC suggestions

**1. New EPIC: "Named scales & swatch labels"**
- Covers: NOTE-0007 (named swatches), NOTE-0021 (custom intervals + drag-along-curve), NOTE-0031 (Tailwind 11 preset), NOTE-0032 (B/W bookends).
- Rationale: All four are facets of "swatches gain identity beyond an index." Designing them together avoids painting yourself into a corner — e.g., Tailwind preset + B/W bookends + custom intervals all need the same `label` field per swatch and consistent count semantics. NOTE-0031 and NOTE-0032 explicitly call out the dependency.
- First step: a planning task to nail down count semantics, label model, and export-label behavior across all four use cases. Then split into per-feature implementation tasks.

**2. New EPIC: "Palette metrics & analysis"**
- Covers: NOTE-0002 (in-context contrast checker), NOTE-0004 (ΔE perceptual scoring).
- Rationale: NOTE-0004 itself states "pairs well with NOTE-0002 (contrast checker) — both are 'metrics layer' features." Both surface measurable color science to the user. Sequencing them together lets you decide whether they share a panel/UI or remain distinct, and lets you build a single metrics utility module (uses culori once EPIC-0009 lands).
- Depends on: EPIC-0009 (culori migration) for clean ΔE/contrast APIs.

**3. New EPIC: "Layout & visual polish"**
- Covers: NOTE-0010 (UI design polish backlog), NOTE-0011 (impeccable critique), NOTE-0018 (swatch copy feedback redesign), NOTE-0027 (swatch hover state), and the font/border-radius items from NOTE-0012.
- Rationale: NOTE-0010 and NOTE-0011 are largely the same five priority issues seen by two different reviewers. Bundling avoids duplicate extraction. The smaller NOTEs (0018, 0027) are sibling polish tweaks that fit the same review cadence.
- First step: extract the five high-overlap items from NOTE-0010+NOTE-0011 as a single batch, then add the small touch-ups.

**4. Existing EPIC-0017 absorbs NOTE-0014, NOTE-0015, NOTE-0022**
- NOTE-0014 (mobile controls dock), NOTE-0015 (explicit value input + steppers), NOTE-0022 (random button).
- Rationale: All three are control-surface affordances. EPIC-0017's stated theme is "control defaults and affordances" with reset/lock/canonical-default work — these are natural siblings. Caveat: NOTE-0014 is responsive-layout-focused; if more mobile/responsive work arrives, split it out.

**5. Existing EPIC-0014 absorbs target-color half of NOTE-0030**
- The target-color review follow-ups (token compliance, focus-visible, aria-label, aria-live, touch support, oklch regex, color formatting) belong with the original target-color epic. The cross-cutting tooling and token-hygiene items (ring-white tokenization, ToggleSwitch knob, port SSOT, ESLint globs, .nvmrc, contrast-audit, CLAUDE.md doc reconciliation) are better as a new "Token & tooling hygiene" EPIC.

**6. Suggested archival/relocation**
- NOTE-0003 (ColorGuesser positioning note) — strategy commentary, not actionable; consider moving to a `references/` or `strategy/` location.
- NOTE-0029 (SSR research) — explicitly superseded by EPIC-0013; could be moved out of `planning/` into an archive.

# EPIC-0018: Named scales & swatch labels

Several proposals all assume that swatches gain identity beyond a numeric index — Tailwind-style shade numbers, named-color labels, custom interval positions, and pure-black/white framing anchors. They share a common substrate: a `label` field per swatch, consistent count semantics, and predictable behavior at export. Designing them in isolation will require rework; this EPIC scopes them as a single coherent feature surface.

## Goals

- Decide once: how do swatch labels work, what does count mean when bookends/presets compose, and how do labels flow into export?
- Ship the named-scale features (Tailwind preset, B/W bookends) on top of that decision rather than as one-off hacks.
- Preserve the option to add per-swatch named-color labels (NOTE-0007) without redesign.

## Tasks

- TASK-0087 — Decide label and count semantics for named scales (planning)
- TASK-0088 — Add `label` field to ColorEntry and propagate through pipeline
- TASK-0089 — Add Tailwind 11-shade preset
- TASK-0090 — Reintroduce B/W bookend swatch toggle

## Source NOTEs

- NOTE-0007 — Named swatches
- NOTE-0021 — Custom swatch intervals
- NOTE-0031 — Tailwind 11-swatch preset
- NOTE-0032 — Bring back B/W bookend swatches

## Related

- EPIC-0017 — Control defaults and affordances. TASK-0079 (range endpoint rework) interacts with B/W bookend semantics.

# EPIC-0019: Palette metrics & analysis

Add a measurable, color-science-backed metrics layer to the palette: WCAG contrast checking between selected swatches, and (eventually) ΔE2000 perceptual scoring of the curve's evenness. Both surface trustable numbers next to the swatches rather than bouncing users out to external tools, and both reinforce "show the math."

## Goals

- Provide WCAG AA/AAA contrast checking for any swatch pair without leaving the app.
- Establish a metrics-utility module (built on culori once EPIC-0009 lands) that contrast and ΔE share.
- Decide where ΔE surfaces — About dialog, swatch hover, badge, or adaptive correction — before building anything user-facing for it.

## Tasks

- TASK-0091 — Decide ΔE application surface (planning)
- TASK-0092 — Implement contrast-ratio and ΔE metrics utility
- TASK-0093 — Design swatch-pair selection UX for contrast comparison
- TASK-0094 — Render inline contrast badge for selected pair

## Source NOTEs

- NOTE-0002 — In-context contrast checker
- NOTE-0004 — ΔE perceptual scoring

## Depends on

- EPIC-0009 — culori migration (cleaner ΔE/contrast APIs).

# TASK-0087: Decide label and count semantics for named scales

Planning task that locks down cross-feature decisions blocking implementation of named scales (Tailwind preset, B/W bookends, custom intervals, named colors).

## Decisions

- **Label model**: optional `label` field on each `ColorEntry`. String only, or structured (kind, value)?
- **Count semantics when features compose**: count = 8 with B/W on → 8 or 10? Tailwind preset (11) + B/W → 13 or override? Custom intervals — labels follow positions?
- **Default labeling**: with no preset active, show numeric labels or stay unlabeled?
- **Export labels**: bookends export as `white`/`black`; Tailwind exports as `gray-50` etc.; custom intervals — by index or position?

## Acceptance

- Decision recorded in this task body or a follow-up NOTE.
- Label data model specified for `ColorEntry`.
- Count semantics specified for: bare, +B/W, +Tailwind, +B/W+Tailwind, +custom intervals.

## Source

- NOTE-0007, NOTE-0021, NOTE-0031, NOTE-0032

# TASK-0093: Design swatch-pair selection UX for contrast comparison

The contrast checker needs an interaction model: how does a user pick two swatches to compare?

## Options

- **Click-to-pin**: one click pins as foreground, second click pins as background; click outside to clear.
- **Hover-pair**: pinned swatch sets foreground; hovering any other swatch shows live ratio against it.
- **Dedicated picker**: explicit foreground/background selectors next to the readout.

A combination is likely — pinned-FG + hover-BG matches palette exploration patterns and feels light-touch.

## Acceptance

- Interaction model decided and documented.
- Mockup or low-fi flow showing pin/clear behavior, readout placement, and edge cases (single swatch, deselected state).

## Source

- NOTE-0002

# TASK-0115: Fix opacity-55 contrast degradation in TargetFormats drifted state

When the target is "drifted" (user has moved hue/sat sliders away from the target value), TargetFormats dims its display via `opacity-55`. That blends unpredictably with the surface color and fails contrast in dark mode. This violates the project rule: never use opacity for text dimming — use explicit dim tokens.

## Acceptance

- Drifted state uses an explicit text token (e.g., `text-text-faint`) instead of opacity.
- Drifted readout meets WCAG AA on the project's dark surface.
- Visual hierarchy still distinguishes drifted from active state.

## Source

- NOTE-0030

## Related

- Project convention: no `white/N` opacity-on-text patterns (`feedback_no_opacity_text`).

```

# TASK-0116: Evaluate manual vertex-position slider override

The hue-aware automatic vertex shift (TASK-0022 in EPIC-0006) handles most cases. NOTE-0005 originally proposed a user-controllable vertex slider — open question whether that's still valuable on top of the automatic version, or whether manual override is unnecessary noise.

## Decisions

- Is the automatic hue-aware vertex sufficient for the use cases NOTE-0005 was solving?
- If a manual override is added, does it take precedence over the auto value, or modulate it?
- Where does the slider live in the UI — alongside the curve editing controls of EPIC-0010, or in an "advanced" disclosure?
- Implementation: piecewise parabola vs. shifted-origin formula.

## Acceptance

- Decision documented in this task body or follow-up NOTE.
- If go-ahead, follow-up implementation tasks created under EPIC-0010.

5	---
6	
7	# EPIC-0017: Control defaults and affordances
8	
9	The curve-shaping controls (hue, saturation, adjustment, lightness range) ship with defaults that don't match the canonical perception-corrected curve, and they offer no clean path back to defaults once the user has tweaked. This makes the *first impression* weaker than it should be (defaults that produce pure black/white endpoints, an unlocked range that drifts asymmetric on the first drag) and the *recovery path* nonexistent (no reset).
10	
11	## Goals
12	
13	- First-load state should reflect the canonical perception-corrected curve — the published default — so a user who opens the app and changes nothing already sees the tool's thesis.
14	- Defaults should bias toward the dominant use case (symmetric range, fully chromatic endpoints), with explicit affordances for the asymmetric/edge cases.
15	- Reset paths exist, are visible only when the control has been modified, and communicate that the default *is* the canonical curve.
16	
17	## Tasks
18	
19	- TASK-0078 — Lock range slider by default
20	- TASK-0079 — Rework lightness range defaults and endpoint handling
21	- TASK-0086 — Add reset-to-default for saturation/adjustment/range
22	
23	## Related
24	
25	- EPIC-0012 — UX discoverability improvements (sibling — discoverability of *what controls do*, vs. this EPIC's focus on *what controls default to and how to recover*).
26	- NOTE-0032 — Bring back B/W bookend swatches (interacts with TASK-0079's endpoint handling).
27	- REFE-0001 — Parabolic saturation curve reference (the canonical "default" lives here).
28

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>

```md
---
name: Verify NOTEs against current code before extracting tasks
description: When extracting tasks from older planning notes, verify the described issue still exists in the codebase or live site before creating a task — old NOTEs may describe problems already fixed
type: feedback
---

When extracting tasks from board NOTE files, **verify each described issue against the current codebase or live site before creating a task**. Old NOTEs frequently describe problems that have since been fixed without the NOTE being updated.

**Why:** During the 2026-05-06 NOTE → TASK extraction pass, I created TASK-0098 ("Add static favicon assets to fix /favicon.ico 404") based on NOTE-0010's polish backlog. Grant pushed back: the favicon was already in place at the production URL and `app/public/` already contained the full favicon set. The board's `archive/` directory showed TASK-0032/0036/0040/0043 had shipped the favicon stack months earlier. The NOTE was stale; the agent's inventory report flagged the item but didn't cross-check, and I didn't verify before extracting. Result: a phantom task that wasted attention to identify and remove.

**How to apply:**
- Older NOTEs (especially polish backlogs and review-style notes) are the highest-risk source — features described as "missing" may already exist.
- Before creating a task from a stale NOTE: grep for the described file/component, check `board/archive/` for prior tasks on the same topic, or curl/browse the live site if the issue is user-visible.
- A subagent inventorying NOTEs should be told explicitly to cross-reference the current codebase, not just summarize the NOTE content.
- When unsure whether an issue still exists, flag it to Grant rather than creating the task speculatively.

```

- Dark-mode-friendly default that works before hydration.
- `useDynamicFavicon` continues to overlay the live palette favicon after hydration without regression.

### Related

- TASK-0068 — verify `useDynamicFavicon` fires after hydration (Astro migration).

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```md
---
name: Board items are archived, never deleted
description: Files under board/ are immutable history — even mistaken or obsolete items move to board/archive/ with a status update and rationale, never get rm'd
type: feedback
---

Board items (tasks, EPICs, ADRs, notes, references) under `board/` are **never deleted with `rm`**. They move to `board/archive/` with a status update (`done`, `obsolete`, `cancelled`, etc.) and a brief rationale of why they're being archived.

**Why:** The board is a project audit trail. Even tasks created in error are useful evidence — they record what was considered, what was found stale, and what was decided. Hard-deleting breaks the trail and removes context that may matter later (e.g., "did we ever look at X? yes, see archive/TASK-XXXX which we marked obsolete because Y"). Grant explicitly corrected me on 2026-05-07 after I `rm`'d TASK-0098 instead of archiving it.

**How to apply:**
- When a task or NOTE turns out to be invalid, stale, or superseded: move it to `board/archive/`, update `status:` in frontmatter to `obsolete`/`cancelled`/`superseded`, add an `archived: YYYY-MM-DD` field, and prepend a short paragraph at the top of the body explaining the archival reason.
- Reference the archived item from the parent EPIC's task list with a parenthetical note (`*(archived YYYY-MM-DD; reason)*`) rather than removing the line.
- Same rule applies to NOTEs that get superseded — see `archive/NOTE-0013_target-color-input.md` for an example pattern.
- The only time `rm` is appropriate is for a file created in the same conversation that hasn't been committed AND the user explicitly asks for hard deletion.

```

ADR status reflects whether the decision still applies, not where it is in a process:
| Status | Meaning |
There is no `proposed` or `pending` status. Decision-making work is tracked as TASKs.
    → ADR written (status: active)
    → Old ADR: status: superseded, superseded_by: [[new ADR]]

| NOTE → TASK | Title must become imperative mood; set `status: backlog` |
| Any → REFE | Strip status field; content must be objective/encyclopedic |
| Any → RESO | Requires a `url` field; set `status: inbox` |
| task | type, status, created |
| epic | type, status, created |
| adr | type, status, context_date, created (spawned items in body) |
| resource | type, status, url, created |
| note | type, status, created (spawned when processed) |

### NOTE (Unrefined Thoughts)

--
                └── Archive the NOTE (status: processed)
                      └── Move to archive/ with spawned: links
```

#### Key Principles
--

2. **Never delete NOTEs** — Even if a NOTE spawns nothing useful, archive it with `status: processed`. The thought process has value as historical context.

3. **One NOTE can spawn many** — A single NOTE might spawn 3 TASKs, 1 REFE, and an ADR. Or it might spawn nothing but still get archived.

4. **Archive after processing** — Once all useful artifacts have been spawned, update `status: processed`, add `spawned:` links, and move to `archive/`.

#### Processed NOTE Example

--
├── resources/       # External material inbox (RESO-*)
└── archive/         # Completed/processed items
```

| Type | Directory |
--
- ADRs in `reference/` (they're project-specific history, not general knowledge)
- Completed items in active directories (archive them)

---

--
1. Update `status` field
2. Move file to `board/archive/` subdirectory
3. Queries automatically exclude archived items (different path)

Archived items remain searchable and linkable.

**ADRs are NOT archived** — they stay in `board/planning/` regardless of status since they are immutable historical records that should remain easily discoverable.

---

--
| Created a NOTE 5 minutes ago, should have been a TASK | **Error correction** | Delete NOTE, create TASK with same content |
| NOTE existed for days, now extracting actionable work from it | **Spawning** | NOTE stays, new TASK(s) created, NOTE gets `spawned:` links and is archived |

The distinction is intent: error correction fixes a mistake; spawning extracts value from a correctly-typed item.

--
   ```bash
   mkdir -p board/{tasks,planning,reference,resources,archive}
   ```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 07:35:53Z)**_

You're right to question it. Spec says tasks only support `backlog | current | done`. "Obsolete" was invented.

There's also a tension I should flag: the spec actually permits hard deletion for *error correction* ("Created a NOTE 5 minutes ago, should have been a TASK → delete and recreate"). So my original `rm` was technically defensible by the spec, but conflicts with your explicit archive-everything rule. Your rule wins.

Cleanest fix within the spec: use `status: done` (the only valid terminal status) and let the body text disambiguate that "done" here means "nothing to do — pre-resolved." Updating the file and the memory note I just wrote.

7	Board items (tasks, EPICs, ADRs, notes, references) under `board/` are **never deleted with `rm`**. They move to `board/archive/` with a status update (`done`, `obsolete`, `cancelled`, etc.) and a brief rationale of why they're being archived.
8	
9	**Why:** The board is a project audit trail. Even tasks created in error are useful evidence — they record what was considered, what was found stale, and what was decided. Hard-deleting breaks the trail and removes context that may matter later (e.g., "did we ever look at X? yes, see archive/TASK-XXXX which we marked obsolete because Y"). Grant explicitly corrected me on 2026-05-07 after I `rm`'d TASK-0098 instead of archiving it.
10	
11	**How to apply:**
12	- When a task or NOTE turns out to be invalid, stale, or superseded: move it to `board/archive/` and add an `archived: YYYY-MM-DD` field, then prepend a short paragraph at the top of the body explaining the archival reason.
13	- **Status field must use a value the spec allows.** Per the board spec at `/Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/spec/board-system-spec.md`:
14	  - TASK: `backlog | current | done` — for archived-but-not-completed tasks, use `done` and disambiguate in the body ("pre-resolved," "filed in error," etc.). Do NOT invent statuses like `obsolete`, `cancelled`, or `wontfix`.
15	  - EPIC: `planning | current | done`
16	  - NOTE: `inbox | processing | processed` — archive uses `processed`
17	  - ADR: `active | superseded | deprecated` (and ADRs stay in `planning/`, never archived)
18	- Reference the archived item from the parent EPIC's task list with a parenthetical note (`*(archived YYYY-MM-DD; reason)*`) rather than removing the line.
19	- The spec does technically permit hard `rm` for narrow *error correction* (e.g., wrong type chosen minutes ago) but Grant's rule overrides this — always archive instead, regardless of how recent.
20
```

Board items (tasks, EPICs, ADRs, notes, references) under `board/` are **never deleted with `rm`**. They move to `board/archive/` with a status update (`done`, `obsolete`, `cancelled`, etc.) and a brief rationale of why they're being archived.

**Why:** The board is a project audit trail. Even tasks created in error are useful evidence — they record what was considered, what was found stale, and what was decided. Hard-deleting breaks the trail and removes context that may matter later (e.g., "did we ever look at X? yes, see archive/TASK-XXXX which we marked obsolete because Y"). Grant explicitly corrected me on 2026-05-07 after I `rm`'d TASK-0098 instead of archiving it.


==> /Users/grant/.claude/projects/-Users-grant-code-chroma-curve/memory/feedback_copy_changes.md <==
---
name: Copy change conservatism
description: Don't over-broaden copy in the About dialog — "neutral" is accurate in specific technical contexts
type: feedback
---

When rebranding, don't rewrite copy where "neutral" is used in a technically accurate context. The About dialog strip descriptions ("Desired neutral midtones...", "too saturated for neutrals") are explaining a specific problem with neutral palettes — changing them would lose precision.

**Why:** Grant rejected proposed rewrites of these strips. The About dialog walks through a problem/solution narrative where neutrals are the motivating example. Broadening that copy dilutes the explanation.


==> /Users/grant/.claude/projects/-Users-grant-code-chroma-curve/memory/feedback_no_opacity_text.md <==
---
name: No opacity for text colors
description: Use explicit color values (e.g. hsl grays) instead of white with opacity for text and UI elements
type: feedback
---

Use explicit color values instead of opacity on text — e.g. `text-[hsl(0_0%_60%)]` not `text-white/60`.

**Why:** Opacity on text makes it blend unpredictably with backgrounds. Grant doesn't prefer this pattern even though the codebase currently uses it — it wasn't intentional, just inherited from early development.


==> /Users/grant/.claude/projects/-Users-grant-code-chroma-curve/memory/feedback_screenshot_directory.md <==
---
name: Save screenshots to .screenshots directory
description: When taking Playwright screenshots in this project, save them to .screenshots/ (gitignored)
type: feedback
---

Save all Playwright screenshots to `.screenshots/` in the project root, not the project root itself.

**Why:** Screenshots are ephemeral working artifacts that clutter the root and shouldn't be committed. The directory is gitignored.


==> /Users/grant/.claude/projects/-Users-grant-code-chroma-curve/memory/feedback_single_source_of_truth.md <==
---
name: Single source of truth over duplication
description: Always use a single source of truth for config values shared across processes — never duplicate, even for "simple" values like port numbers
type: feedback
originSessionId: a2d94174-6482-4acf-97a0-736d38bf6197
---
Never duplicate config values across files, even when duplication seems harmless. A small amount of extra planning to establish a single source of truth is always worth it.

**Why:** Agentic coding workflows surface this issue more than solo human dev. Two processes (AI agent and human in terminal) need to read the same config (dev server port, log paths, etc.). Duplication creates silent drift that's harder for an agent to detect than a human who "just knows."


==> /Users/grant/.claude/projects/-Users-grant-code-chroma-curve/memory/feedback_subagent_tool_preferences.md <==
---
name: Enforce rg/fd in subagent prompts
description: When dispatching Explore/general-purpose subagents, explicitly require rg over grep and fd over find, matching the global CLAUDE.md preference
type: feedback
originSessionId: bff81298-8194-4ecd-85f4-80e4b86ac404
---
When spawning subagents (Explore, general-purpose, etc.), pass an explicit instruction in the prompt to use `rg` over `grep` and `fd` over `find`. Subagents do not inherit tool-choice preferences from the parent's global CLAUDE.md rules; they'll reach for `grep`/`find` by default unless told otherwise.

**Why:** Grant's global CLAUDE.md mandates `rg`/`fd` over `grep`/`find`. He caught a subagent in the chroma-curve project running `grep -n ... | head -10` and `find ... -name "index.ts"` and flagged it as a rule violation even though I hadn't run those shell commands directly. Delegated work still has to honor the project's tool-preference rules.


==> /Users/grant/.claude/projects/-Users-grant-code-chroma-curve/memory/feedback_verify_stale_notes.md <==

name: Verify NOTEs against current code before extracting tasks

type: feedback
originSessionId: 309b068b-0dc5-4e81-8948-3c013d363a1b
---
When extracting tasks from board NOTE files, **verify each described issue against the current codebase or live site before creating a task**. Old NOTEs frequently describe problems that have since been fixed without the NOTE being updated.
